items.title: Antarctica's first dinosaur fossil found in drawer after 40 years

children.content: A bone found in Antarctica in 1985, and kept in a drawer in England for almost four decades, turns out to belong to one of the largest types of dinosaurs to ever walk the Earth.

gallery.altText: The fossilised dinosaur bone placed next to geologist Dr Mike Thomson's journal recording the discovery.
A scientist in an orange Antarctic suit stands next to an orange tent in a grey Antarctic landscape with blue skies.
An artist recreation of the Antarctic-sauropod, a long-necked dinosaur, amongst prehistoric plants.

thumbnail.altText: The fossilised dinosaur bone placed next to geologist Dr Mike Thomson's journal recording the discovery.
A scientist in an orange Antarctic suit stands next to an orange tent in a grey Antarctic landscape with blue skies.
An artist recreation of the Antarctic-sauropod, a long-necked dinosaur, amongst prehistoric plants.

items.title: Five tonnes of antibiotics used to combat disease in salmon

children.content: More than five tonnes of antibiotics were used to treat a disease that killed 9,000 tonnes of fish last summer, Salmon Tasmania says.

image.altText: Atlantic salmon in a fish farming pen, Tasmania

items.title: Pair get temporary ban on keeping dogs after RSPCA inspection

children.content: Two people have been convicted and disqualified from owning dogs for five years after a severely emaciated dog was rescued by RSPCA inspectors and nursed back to health.

image.altText: A dog looks at the camera.

items.title: Paid pathway aims to lure career changers into the classroom

children.content: Lauren Shivvaan worked in law and the public service before making a life-changing career decision in her late 30s.

image.altText: Lauren Shivvaan

items.title: LISTEN: New program launched to find teachers with 'diverse experience', and will pay you to train

children.content: The Change2Teaching Program has been launched which aims to find teachers with different backgrounds and life experience, and will pay a salary during training.

image.altText: A teacher in front of a classroom, seen from behind.

items.title: 'No place for the poison of antisemitism': New warning for universities

children.content: The government's Antisemitism Education Taskforce, led by David Gonski, has equipped teachers with new resources to tackle antisemitism and promote "Australian values".

articles.title: MP Josh Burns reveals toll of online abuse after office vandalised

children.content: Federal Victorian MP Josh Burns details an attack on his electorate office where a hammer was used to smash the windows, and the following abuse on social media.

image.altText: Joshua Burns in suit and blue tie wearing glasses outside royal commission

articles.title: Lives would have been saved if armed Jewish guards were at Bondi, Lowy says

children.content: Prominent Jewish businessman and philanthropist Steven Lowy says the "tragedy would have been far smaller" if armed security were at the event targeted in the Bondi Beach shooting.

image.altText: Man in suit behind microphone, grey background

gallery.altText: a wide image of sandstone arches at a university campus. people can be seen walking alongside the arches

thumbnail.altText: a wide image of sandstone arches at a university campus. people can be seen walking alongside the arches

items.title: As Trump plays dealmaker-in-chief, the reality on the ground is very different

children.content: Two recent Middle East deals, one in Iran and the other in Lebanon, seem at odds with each other, and show how the Trump administration is running different approaches depending on who it is talking to at any given moment.

image.altText: Benjamin Netanyah dressed in black walks among a crowd of people.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit stands in a corridor of parliament house.

items.title: Childcare centre that exposed kids to rat poison stripped of funding

children.content: The Melbourne childcare provider has been cut off under new powers that allow the federal government to withdraw funding due to safety breaches.

image.altText: Kids toys on the floor.

items.title: Menswear brand accused of using AI to 'whitewash' model's image

children.content: Australian model Elii Emeghebo is pursuing a racial discrimination complaint against suit label Peter Jackson, claiming AI has been used to "whitewash" his image.

gallery.altText: Two images of a male model side by side, one in colour and one in black and white

thumbnail.altText: Two images of a male model side by side, one in colour and one in black and white

avatar.alt: A journalist with dark hair, smiling at the camera.

items.title: Liberal MP suspended from parliament for 24 hours after QT outburst

children.content: Liberal MP Garth Hamilton has been suspended from the House of Representatives for 24 hours after an outburst during Question Time.

image.altText: A man in a grey suit opens a door to leave the house of representatives.

items.title: These sexual enhancement supplements contain hidden prescription drugs

children.content: The TGA issues a warning for consumers using sexual enhancement products after an investigation finds several products contain undeclared prescription drugs that could cause serious side effects.

image.altText: A package titled Lustro is shown. It is orange and has a man and woman laughing on the package.

items.title: Lifeless Germany fall at World Cup as Paraguay show tenacity to die another day

children.content: Paraguay's upset penalty-shootout win over Germany at the World Cup, while stunning, was not entirely unexpected, as they showed impressive determination to die another day.

articles.title: Quick hits: Shootout drama, VAR controversy make for German nightmare

children.content: The knockout stages of the World Cup are in full swing, with penalty shootout drama and VAR controversy already featuring. Here are today's quick hits.

image.altText: Kai Havertz, German fan and Gustavo Gomez

articles.title: How a punt on a trial in Italy created the Socceroos' next star

children.content: Cristian Volpato was rejected by two Australian soccer academies. His family dropped everything to go for a trial in Italy. That was the making of Australia's next star.

image.altText: Cristian Volpato #20 of Australia strikes the ball during the FIFA World Cup

gallery.altText: Orlando Gill screams towards the crowd while players celebrate behind him
Paraguay players run and scream in delight after winning

items.title: Uni's HR chief tells ICAC she was 'shocked' by promotion proposal

children.content: Corruption watchdog hears University of Wollongong Chancellor Michael Still rejected calls to advertise a proposed executive role for chief governance officer.

image.altText: Sign with large circles saying UPOW

items.title: Paediatrician accused of child sex offences now facing 53 charges

children.content: A Perth paediatrician accused of using hidden cameras to record himself abusing children is now facing more than 50 charges involving 10 alleged victims, with a court told further charges are likely to be laid.

image.altText: A head and shoulders shot of an Asian-Australian man, with black hair, wearing spectacles and a blue and white business shirt.

items.title: From 'dull and deluded' to 'a peppy, hot pink delight': What critics think of Elle

children.content: Legally Blonde was an overwhelming hit, so what do critics think of the prequel Elle?

gallery.altText: Lexi Minetree as Elle with long blonde hair, wearing pink sitting at a desk in a classroom
Lexi Minetree as Elle sitting on a pink shawl outside reading a magazine surrounded by 90s memorabilia

items.title: Don't ask me what's culturally appropriate, I don't know either

children.content: After sending out invitations to a belated elopement party, I was suddenly fielding calls from friends who wanted to wear saris but were worried that it might be disrespectful.

image.altText: Sashi Perera stands next to her husband Charlie, wearing a burnt orange sari. Charlie wears a suit.

avatar.alt: Portrait of writer and comedian Sashi Perera wearing a long shirt dress that is pale purple, with a green skirt.

items.title: 'One of the greatest Antarctic collections in the world' on display in Canberra

children.content: For most, a trip to Antarctica is well beyond reach, but a new exhibition at the National Museum of Australia gives visitors the chance to explore life in one of the most extreme environments on Earth.

image.altText: Five taxidermied sled dogs on display at a museum exhibition about Antarctica.

items.title: Whale census records unexplained drop in sightings off WA's South West

children.content: Marine scientists have expressed concerns following a 60 per cent drop in migrating whales seen off South West WA's coastline compared to previous years.

gallery.altText: A humpback whale showing its white underbelly as it breaches out of the water.

thumbnail.altText: A humpback whale showing its white underbelly as it breaches out of the water.

items.title: Ernst and Young staff sacked as Albanese's banking information allegedly breached

image.altText: A man wearing a suit with a coat, a red tie and glasses.

children.content: Two Ernst and Young employees have been sacked after Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's personal banking information was allegedly breached while the pair worked for the accounting firm at the Commonwealth Bank.

items.title: Rex Airlines breached continuous disclosure obligations

children.content: Rex contravened its continuous disclosure obligations and misled the market, the NSW Supreme Court found on Tuesday.

image.altText: a plane flies in the sky

items.title: Bid to make scam texts easier to detect set to begin

children.content: The changes will come into effect tomorrow and small businesses will need to get in touch with their telco.

image.altText: Text messages from scammers posing as organisations will be labelled as 'unverified'.

items.title: The government is making major changes to universities. Here's what to expect

children.content: The government will spend an extra $3.6 billion over a decade on new university places, but some are warning the money could be wasted.

image.altText: University generic showing a close up of a sign saying Uni on a building.

items.title: Suspected saltwater crocodile takes dog at unexpected outback location

children.content: Richmond Shire Mayor John Wharton believes a dog has been taken by a saltwater crocodile in the Flinders River, hundreds of kilometres inland from its usual habitat.

image.altText: The Flinders River at the Bottom Crossing near Richmond.

items.title: North-west Victorian sporting league adopts new concussion app

children.content: A sporting league in regional Victoria is the first to adopt a concussion app aimed at improving communication and accountability around concussions at grassroots sports.

articles.title: 'We don't fully understand': The footy risk few parents grasp

children.content: Contact is an essential part of footy, but there is growing evidence it can also be a grave threat to the health and welfare of its players. Experts say the AFL should be acting now to protect current and future players.

image.altText: Players compete for the ball at a junior Australian Rules football match.

avatar.alt: Portrait of ABC journalist Jessica Halloran.

items.title: Why Telstra, Optus and TPG mobile coverage maps now look different

children.content: Australia's major telcos are being forced to comply with new standards when promoting mobile coverage networks, and Telstra is not happy.

image.altText: Deb Bewick holds a mobile phone up in search of reception

items.title: Brisbane protester removed from tree after 23 hours

children.content: Emergency services had to remove a power line to lift the woman down from the tree at Kurilpa Common, a state-owned lot taken over by "guerilla gardeners" during COVID-19.

image.altText: A woman wearing a brown shirt and red skirt sits on the limb of a tree.

items.title: Seven Australians in Venezuela request government help as quake toll climbs

children.content: Australia is providing "consular assistance" to seven people after Venezuela's two earthquakes, Foreign Affairs Minister Penny Wong confirms.
